description: Thin style wrapper over the native <input> element. All accessibility characteristics come from the native input: implicit role based on type (textbox, searchbox, checkbox-like for type="file", etc.), built-in keyboard behavior, and native disabled semantics.

keyboard.action: Moves focus to / from the input.
Jump to start / end of the value.

focus.notes: Uses focus-visible:ring-1 for keyboard focus. Disabled inputs show cursor-not-allowed and opacity-50; they remain in the accessibility tree but are skipped by Tab.

announcements: Announces with its label (via <Label htmlFor> or wrapping <label>), type-derived role, and current value.
Placeholder text is announced as a hint by most modern AT, not as the value.
When aria-invalid is true, announces "invalid" with the linked aria-describedby error message.
screenReader.notes: Never rely on placeholder as a label; it disappears once the user starts typing and is not reliably announced. Use the Label component or aria-label / aria-labelledby.

contrast.text: Value uses foreground, placeholder uses text-muted-foreground. Both meet WCAG AA against bg-transparent
contrast.ui: Border uses border-input; focus ring uses ring-ring. Both meet AA non-text contrast

motion.reducedMotion: Only a color transition on focus / blur; no motion-based animations

consumerResponsibilities: Always provide a real label via <Label htmlFor> or aria-label; placeholder alone is not accessible.
Set type correctly (email, tel, url, search, number) so mobile keyboards and AT match the expected input.
Pair with aria-describedby when you show helper text or errors; the component does not wire this automatically (use FormControl for that).
